patch.value: CONTEXT: the guiding principle is 'project management = context management'; agents gating on pm health/validate need machine-executable next actions, not just human prose. DECISION: maintain a single source-of-truth remediation registry (src/core/diagnostics/remediation.ts) mapping each non-extension health/validate check code to a machine-executable remediation command; surface it as a per-check remediation_map on 'pm health --json' and as 'pm validate --fix-hints'. The resolver is FIRST-MATCH over disjoint check codes (not longest-prefix), so there is no dead tie-break branch and the module stays 100% covered. CONSEQUENCES: agents can self-repair deterministically; remediation guidance is centralized and testable; adding a check requires a registry entry; the resolver must remain first-match to preserve coverage. EVIDENCE: src/core/diagnostics/remediation.ts; closed pm-0hnu (health remediation_map), pm-6m3y (validate --fix-hints), pm-7tcw (settings_read warning surfacing); related ADR pm-jezo (advisory vs blocking health).
ADR: Agent self-repair via a centralized remediation registry surfaced through pm health --json and pm validate --fix-hints
Agent self-repair via a centralized remediation registry surfaced through pm health --json and pm validate --fix-hints
Recorded as an ADR for a decision already in effect; appended to the living ADR log (pm-m2u1).
